items.definition_en: An airport is a place where planes take off and land.
items.example_en: We arrived at the airport two hours before the flight.

items.definition_en: A beach is a sandy or rocky area next to water.
items.example_en: They spent the whole day at the beach.

items.definition_en: A mountain is a very high hill.
items.example_en: We climbed a mountain during our trip.

items.definition_en: A city is a large town where many people live.
items.example_en: London is a big city with many attractions.

items.definition_en: An island is a piece of land surrounded by water.
items.example_en: They traveled to a small island for vacation.

items.definition_en: A market is a place where people buy and sell things.
items.example_en: We visited the local market every morning.

items.definition_en: A museum is a place where you can see old or interesting things.
items.example_en: The art museum was full of beautiful paintings.

items.definition_en: A park is a green area in a city for people to enjoy.
items.example_en: Children played in the park after school.

items.definition_en: A castle is a large building where kings or queens used to live.
items.example_en: They visited an ancient castle during their trip.

items.definition_en: A forest is a large area filled with trees.
items.example_en: We walked through the forest in the morning.

items.definition_en: A desert is a very dry area with a lot of sand.
items.example_en: Camels live in the desert.

items.definition_en: A temple is a building used for religious activities.
items.example_en: They took photos of the old temple.

items.definition_en: A square is an open area in a town where people meet.
items.example_en: The main square is full of cafes and shops.

items.definition_en: A harbor is a place for boats and ships to stay.
items.example_en: We ate at a restaurant right next to the harbor.

items.definition_en: A bridge is a structure built to cross over rivers or roads.
items.example_en: We walked across the old stone bridge.

items.definition_en: A monument is a statue or building that honors a person or event.
items.example_en: They stopped to look at the war monument.

items.definition_en: A palace is a large, grand building where kings or queens live.
items.example_en: The royal family lives in a large palace.

items.definition_en: A village is a small group of houses in the countryside.
items.example_en: We spent the night in a quiet village.

items.definition_en: A waterfall is water that falls from a height.
items.example_en: The view of the waterfall was amazing.

items.definition_en: A zoo is a place where people can see wild animals.
items.example_en: We saw many animals at the zoo.
